vehicle.label: Force-Feedback Haptic Glove Pair
vehicle.desc: Wearable force-feedback exo-glove pair for VR grasp resistance, serviceable actuator cartridges, linkages, wrist band, and dorsal control module

parts.spec: Knuckle-crossing wiring loom with service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, flex-rated bend radius, power vs signal channel separation

parts.spec: Dorsal knuckle row mounting rail, actuator plug-in pockets, datum pads per finger station, fastener pattern and removal path

risk.desc: Insufficient thermal management may lead to overheating.
risk.mit: Increase heat sink size or add active cooling.

risk.desc: Toolless lid may not provide adequate protection for internal components.
risk.mit: Add additional sealing or protective measures around the lid.

risk.desc: Cable exit may not be properly sealed, leading to potential ingress of contaminants.
risk.mit: Implement a grommet or similar sealing mechanism at the cable exit.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interlocking parts.
risk.mit: Implement alignment guides and ensure clear assembly instructions.

risk.desc: Risk of reduced durability if strain relief loops are not properly integrated.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough testing of strain relief implementation before production.

risk.desc: User discomfort if ergonomic padding is not correctly positioned.
risk.mit: Prototype with user feedback to refine ergonomic padding placement.

risk.desc: Possible interference between finger cartridge and adjacent components if clearance is not maintained.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearance dimensions as necessary during design review.

risk.desc: Risk of improper actuator module insertion if service port is not clearly marked.
risk.mit: Ensure service port is prominently labeled and easily accessible.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Two-segment exoskeleton finger linkage cartridge, single-pin removable, hinge datum pads, thimble slip interface

physics_paths.path_description: Four-bar linkage motion from actuator module to finger tip
Heat dissipation from actuator module through finger cartridge
Service access for actuator module replacement

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate force sensors within finger cartridge for precise haptic feedback
Implement strain relief loops for wiring at flexion points
Incorporate ergonomic padding around finger cartridge for user comfort

verification_focus.description: Ensure total mass does not exceed 20g
Verify hinge pins are securely inserted with proper preload
Confirm ease of actuator module replacement via service port

generated_parts.label: Flexion-Resistance Actuator x 10
generated_parts.spec: Plug-in tendon brake/clutch actuator module, knuckle row mount, cable pulley, connector pocket, service access

requirements: Plug-in tendon brake/clutch actuator module, knuckle row mount, cable pulley, connector pocket, service access
Serviceable actuator cartridges, linkages, wrist band, and dorsal control module
Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access
Actuators are visible near knuckle row and remain removable without full teardown
Cables are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance
Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles

verify.rule: The subsystem's estimated mass is 45g, which is below the 50g limit.
All critical joints have visible locking washers.
Connectors are accessible without disassembly.
All required evidence features are present in the part_tree/geometry_ops.
Each physical feature in the geometry_ops is traceable back to its corresponding internal feature in the part_tree.
Adjacent interfaces are named with exact neighboring subsystem labels and connection details are reciprocal.
Critical joints have visible locking washers.

risk.desc: Potential over-budget mass if additional sensors or components are added.
risk.mit: Monitor mass during design iterations and adjust component choices as necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of connector mis-mating due to lack of keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Implement keying or labeling on connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of harness strain if bend radius is not respected.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry and routing respects the stated minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: Risk of service access being obstructed if not properly represented in geometry.
risk.mit: Ensure service access is represented by geometry such as hatches, pockets, or channels.

risk.desc: Risk of joint failure if preload intent is not met.
risk.mit: Verify that all critical joints meet the preload intent and have visible locking evidence.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Plug-in tendon brake/clutch actuator module, knuckle row mount, cable pulley, connector pocket, service access

physics_paths.description: Hand force is transmitted to the actuator module via tendon cables.
Hand movement causes the cable pulley to rotate, adjusting the tension on the tendon cables.
Heat from the environment is dissipated through the actuator module housing.

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate strain gauges within the actuator module to monitor force feedback.
Route data cables from the actuator module to the connector pocket.
Provide a service access port for maintenance of the actuator module.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the subsystem does not exceed the weight limit of 50g.
Verify all critical joints have visible locking evidence and meet preload intent.
Confirm all connectors are accessible without disassembly.

risk.desc: Potential fatigue failure due to lack of strain relief in wiring.
risk.mit: Implement service loops and strain relief at every flex boundary.

risk.desc: Risk of improper grounding if grounding scheme is not clearly defined.
risk.mit: Specify grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and ensure signal returns are routed alongside signals.

risk.desc: Wearable comfort may be compromised if rigid shell ignores don/doff, comfort, and finger clearance.
risk.mit: Ensure rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance.

risk.desc: Actuators may be inaccessible if they are bonded or hidden without connector/service geometry.
risk.mit: Ensure actuators plug in and remain visible near knuckle row.

risk.desc: Power and signal wires sharing one tight bundle along the whole route may cause interference.
risk.mit: Separate motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines in different channels or cross them at right angles.
